### Post by Jason_25 on 2006-02-23
It would also be good for you to post your Xorg log.  Since reviewing that would be the primary troubleshooting method.

One problem that I see is that your second monitor only supports 1024x768 and your metamodes specify 1280x1024.  X will try to use the highest resolution possible.

---

### Post by thomasando on 2006-02-23
There's quite a few things you can try. First off, I'm assuming that you have installed the nvidia-glx extension package? If not and you cant install it, it's not a big deal, we can get around that and install/enable glx later. If you want to disable glx, change the following line (comment it out):
```
    Load           "glx"
```
To:
```
    #Load           "glx"
```

Secondly, your video card is expecting 2 digital flat panel monitors to be connected (DFP). Going by the link you provided, it looks like your second monitor isn't DFP but is a CRT. Make sure this line is specified correctly. For example, I have 2 LCD monitors on my box, but they both have analog D-Sub connectors, and plug in to a regular VGA port. My nvidia card has a D-Sub port and a DVI port. I have a DVI-DSub adapter on my second monitor. I have specified both my monitors as follows (because they both use DSub connectors and I dont need the card to output in DVI mode):
```
Option		"ConnectedMonitor" "crt, crt"
```
Check what kind of monitors you have and adjust this line to suit.

Last off, you could try to get it running using a lower resolution and lower colour bitrate. Change your 'Default Depth' to 16 (for 16-bit) and add an extra entry at the start of the MetaModes line like this:
```
Option		"Metamodes" "800x600,800x600;1280x1024,1024x768; 1024x768,1024x768; 1280x1024,NULL; 1024x768,NULL"
```
It will look terrible but both monitors should support that mode.

You could also try commenting out this line:
```
Option		"AllowGLXWithComposite"
```

Let me know how you get on. Hopefully this will make your second monitor jump to life.

---

### Post by thomasando on 2006-02-23
[QUOTE=Jason_25]
One problem that I see is that your second monitor only supports 1024x768 and your metamodes specify 1280x1024.  X will try to use the highest resolution possible.[/QUOTE]

Nope - the first monitor will use 1280x1024. The second monitor will use the part after the comma - so 1024x768. If the Twinview Orientation option was set to 'Left Of' it would work the way you suggested, but because it's on the right, it uses the mode to the right of the comma.

---

### Post by thomasando on 2006-02-23
The HDTV is DVI out. Do you have an adapter connected to this port to hook up one of the monitors? If you do, you'll want to use CRT mode for that monitor. If there's no adapter, then in all likelihood you will want to use dfp. The regular VGA port will need to be set to CRT I believe. On my card, the DVI port (with adapter) is the primary display, the VGA port is the secondary.

Both monitors, are they flat panel displays, or are they deep and have a glass tube like a TV? I suspect that if you were to change the dfp,dfp options to crt,crt that you would likely see some sort of reliable activity.

What do you mean when you say you can sort of see the second screen on the logon screen? What you want is a good picture on it, and to be able to move the mouse over to the other screen and see the cursor moving around... are you using ubuntu or kubuntu?


You may also want to modify the Xinerama option to be 'On', like so:
```
Option "Xinerama" "On"
```
That will enable the X.org server multiple head support.

---
